notes: kiro-cli has no auto-classifier; it prompts for EVERY write/shell by default (read-only auto-runs). This cell exercises the RISKY arc the scenario asserts — a mutating tool call that raises kiro's 'write requires approval' picker (Yes, single permission / Trust, always allow in this session; FINDINGS.md §6). The recipe launches plain `kiro-cli chat` (NO --trust-all-tools), asks kiro to write a file (so the picker appears), holds the picker open past the ~5s OpenToolStalled stall window, drives it via `keys` Enter to select 'Yes, single permission', and the turn completes — recording the full arc ready→working→waiting→working→ready. PASSES as of ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#588 (PR #612, merged 2026-06-05): the daemon's permission-gated edit-tool match (isPermissionGatedEditTool) is now case-insensitive, so kiro's lowercase `write` is recognized, OpenToolStalled (rule 1b) fires while the picker is pending, and the session reaches waiting (events.jsonl reason 'stalled edit tool → likely permission prompt → waiting'). Previously known_failing: the exact-name allowlist Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit missed lowercase `write`, so the pending-approval window stayed working. The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only, not read by the v1 parser; FINDINGS.md §4), so the model field is EMPTY (omitted from observations, matching the recorded 2-x kiro cells) and cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false. 2.6.0 prereq nuance: this cell requires chat.disableTrustAllConfirmation=false in ~/.kiro/settings/cli.json, conflicting with the trust-all consent other kiro cells persist (the dispatcher flips it for this run and restores it afterwards).

text: kiro-cli chat session appears in ready
User prompt asking kiro to write a file drives ready → working

The write-approval picker is pending — the open `write` toolUse is in the transcript with no result until approval, so the session is waiting. The daemon reaches this via OpenToolStalled (rule 1b): isPermissionGatedEditTool now matches kiro's lowercase `write` case-insensitively (#588 / PR #612), so the held edit-permission classifies as waiting (events.jsonl reason 'stalled edit tool → likely permission prompt → waiting').
User answers the picker ('Yes') → the write tool executes and emits its toolResult → waiting → working
Turn completes back to ready (text-only AssistantMessage); final state is ready
